spec_match.rationale: Models the spec rule exactly: source_debug_key and trigger_debug_key included only when both ar_debug cookies are set and the report is not cross-app/web. Strips otherwise.

interactive.rationale: Three selects (source cookie, trigger cookie, context) plus two text inputs drive a live JSON report and verdict line.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb promises toggle cookie state, watch debug keys get stripped — page delivers.

edge_cases.rationale: Cross-app/web branch is handled (good) but does not model the case where source_debug_key is registered without trigger_debug_key (or vice versa) — the spec treats the missing-pair case differently from the both-present-but-cookie-blocked case. Also doesn't model the case where the reporting origin and cookie origin differ.

design_system.rationale: All CSS variables, warn-block uses --accent-amber correctly, no static-card.

openQuestions.title: Add the partial-debug-key scenario
openQuestions.detail: What happens if the registration includes only one of source_debug_key or trigger_debug_key? The spec treats this case (debug keys stripped if not both present from the start) similarly but for a different reason. Add a 'register one only' control so the visitor can see how the API handles the asymmetric case.

openQuestions.title: Add a verbose-debug-report counterfactual
openQuestions.detail: Attribution Reporting also has verbose debug reports (separate from the attribution report itself) which have their own debug-key inclusion rules. Either mention they exist or add a third report panel that emits the verbose-debug shape so the reader knows the change applies to two report types.
